title: What does it mean to be a good citizen: level 2
description: Test your knowledge on how good citizens act in civil society!
topic_path: What does it mean to be a citizen?
What does it mean to be a good citizen: level 2

assessment_items.question_content:
To prepare for an incoming hurricane, Amaya goes to the store to buy some sandbags to protect her house from water damage. She’s identified all of the places where water can get in and calculated she’ll only need six sandbags. Despite being afraid about potential damage to her house, Amaya only buys the six sandbags she needs to make sure other people get what they need. 

**Which civic virtue is Amaya demonstrating in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

On his drive to school, Zaire has noticed trash alongside the road. It bothers him that no one is picking it up because it could potentially hurt the animals living in the area. He reaches out to some of his friends from school and they decide to get together that weekend to dispose of the trash. 

**Which civic virtue is Zaire demonstrating in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In their civics class, Ha-yoon and her classmate, Luciana, debate whether the government should tax soft drinks and candy to promote more nutritious habits. Ha-yoon thinks that the government has a responsibility to protect its citizens and should tax unhealthy foods and drinks to improve overall health. Luciana thinks that any taxes on foods and drinks gives the government too much power and discourages individual choice. At the end of their debate, Ha-yoon and Luciana shake hands and agree to disagree. 

**Which civic virtue are Ha-yoon and Luciana demonstrating in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Emilio loves to paint. One day, while hanging out at the local community center, Emilio noticed that the paint was chipping on one of the exterior walls. He asked the supervisor if he could paint a mural on that wall to highlight some of the great parts of their community. He worked for three weeks for free to spray paint a mural of kids playing sports, dancing, and playing musical instruments. 

**Which civic virtue is Emilio demonstrating in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Fatima is on the honor council at her school, which means whenever there is a question of student wrongdoing, she hears a case from both sides. Her friend, Freya, has been accused of plagiarizing an essay from the internet. Even though she knows that Freya is a good person, once she’s heard all of the evidence, she votes to give Freya a failing grade on the assignment and a week’s worth of detention, the standard punishment. 

**Which civic virtue is Fatima demonstrating in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Hasan is doing some last minute studying before his final test for his government class. His friend, Stefan, runs up to Hasan, looking panicked. He tells Hasan that he forgot the test was today and did not study beforehand. He asks Hasan if he can copy his work during the test so that he does not fail. Hasan wants to help his friend, but he knows it would be wrong to cheat. Hasan tells Stefan that he can’t help him, but he offers to help him cram for the test beforehand. 

**Which civic virtue is Hasan demonstrating in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Reagan wants to start an organization at their school where current high schoolers can tutor students at their neighboring elementary school. Reagan’s principal, Mr. Andrada, is concerned that it would take too much effort to get the club up and running and encourages Reagan to join a club instead. Reagan asks Mr. Andrada for a list of things they would need to accomplish and works for weeks until they check all the tasks off the list with the help of their friends. Mr. Andrada approves the club. 

**Which civic virtue is Reagan demonstrating in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which activity is an example of civil society rather than government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A local Habitat for Humanity chapter organizes an event to build homes for people in their community after a major fire.
The school board for the Star City Unified School District creates a new civics curriculum for the high schools in their district.
Mayor Wilson Fisk and Mayor Jay Garrick argue in a televised debate over the role of local governments in enforcing immigration laws.
Gateway City shuts down several major roads to escort the president to her speech at the Town Hall.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which activity is an example of civil society rather than government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: After several instances of teens kicking over garbage cans, Adnan’s parents set up a neighborhood watch with a few of their neighbors.
President Molonoski tasks the Department of Transportation to implement a policy meant to improve national highways.
Congresswoman Da Silva meets with the other members of the House Committee on Veteran Affairs to discuss provisions on a bill.
Senator Tan filibusters a bill to keep the Senate from voting on it because she fears it will negatively affect the farmers in her state.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which activity is an example of civil society rather than the economic market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Orla joined her local chapter of the Society of Professional Journalists after becoming a journalist.
Alisha’s employer pays for her and her family’s health insurance as part of the contract she signed when she agreed to work for them.
Roman gets a formal license from his county office in order to sell tacos from his food truck.
Jabari has to let go of two of his employees after an economic downturn makes it impossible for his business to continue to operate.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which activity is an example of government rather than civil society?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Supreme Court rules on the constitutionality of a presidential executive order meant to limit the sale of goods from certain countries.
The United Auto Workers host a meeting for non-union workers to explain the benefits of joining a union.
Amnesty International creates a campaign to encourage governments around the world to accept refugees.
Jie’s parents discuss which candidates they are voting for in the upcoming midterm election.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which activity is an example of government rather than politics?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The House of Representatives formally impeaches President Miller after he tried to bribe members of the Supreme Court.
Nneka Abdou meets with several potential donors for her campaign for Michigan state senate.
Mateo attends a town hall to meet with the representative of his congressional district.
Ruka writes a letter to the Supreme Court explaining how the outcome of a case on their docket will affect people in her community.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which activity is an example of politics rather than government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congressman Gruber and Congresswoman Ngo agree to trade votes to get the other to support their own legislation.
Senator Mori meets with members of an environmental interest group to understand the impact a recent fracking bill will have on the environment.
The voters in the state of Arkansas elect Edward Anderson to be the new governor for their state.
Officer Kim arrests a man for breaking into a grocery store in the middle of the night.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which activity is an example of politics rather than government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: David and his friends organize a public sit-in in his senator’s office to pressure her to support disability rights legislation.
The voters in the state of Arizona vote in favor of an initiative that would legalize the sale and use of marijuana.
The FBI investigates a potential domestic terrorist group by surveilling their behaviors.
President Garcia signs a bill that would make veterans eligible for certain National Science Foundation programs.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two candidates are running for mayor of Angel Grove. It’s a close race between Rocky DeSantos and Aisha Campbell, but the voters ultimately select Ms. Campbell to be the new mayor of Angel Grove.

**This scenario is an example of which democratic principle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

During the coronavirus crisis, the president made recommendations about how states should handle the situation. However, it’s up to the states to provide for the public health and safety of its residents. Each state set their own measures and had different approaches to stay-at-home orders, school closures, and access to public spaces.

**This scenario is an example of which democratic principle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Before their soccer match, Himari and her teammates take a moment to pray for good favor. Her teammates, Nadia and Sophie, decide not to participate in the prayer, but they stay silent to respect their teammates.

**This scenario is an example of which democratic principle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Teachers in Central City organize a strike after the state refuses to maintain their promise to increase teacher salaries in the 2020-2021 school year. They march on public streets, holding signs that say things like “On strike for better schools” and “Stand with the Central City Teachers Union.”  

**This scenario is an example of which democratic principle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The Kansas state legislature passed a bill requiring that all high schools in the state be renamed in honor of Clark Kent, a fictional superhero. The governor of Kansas used her power of the veto to stop the bill from being signed into law. 

**This scenario is an example of which democratic principle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

As a reward for having perfect attendance for nine weeks, Mrs. Li promises to buy her homeroom class lunch as a reward. The class votes on what they would like and nearly the entire class votes for pizza. The three students who didn’t vote for pizza, Sanaa, Carter, and Karim, are vegan and won’t be able to eat pizza since there are no vegan alternatives at the local pizza shop. Mrs. Li wants to make the majority of her students happy, but she also doesn’t want to leave out the other three kids. As a compromise, she promises to go to a pizza shop in the next town over and get three vegan pasta dishes for Sanaa, Carter, and Karim.

**This scenario is an example of which democratic principle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Detective Jake Peralta arrests Trevor, a teenager who has been spray painting vulgar messages on cars. After Detective Peralta finds out that Trevor’s dad is the Deputy Commissioner of the New York Police Department, the Deputy Commissioner encourages him to drop the case or endanger his career. Detective Peralta does not think it’s fair to give Trevor special treatment just because of who his dad is, so he pursues the case.

**This scenario is an example of which democratic principle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
